meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't deploy apps. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit: Deploy Your First App Like a Pro
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with deployment. Use this SaaS starter kit guide to launch your first project using Antigravity and Claude Code. Perfect for students.
content.answer_block: Deployment paralysis is a rite of passage for every developer. By using a pre-configured SaaS starter kit, you bypass infrastructure configuration and focus on shipping code. Leverage Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for automated debugging to transform your local prototype into a live, production-ready application within hours.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks perfecting your logic, tweaking your UI, and polishing your database schema. Yet, when you attempt to push to production, you are met with a wall of cryptic environment errors, broken build pipelines, and server-side timeouts. The dream of launching your first SaaS feels like it is slipping away as you drown in DevOps complexity.

This is the threshold where most students quit. But the gap between a local project and a live application isn't talent—it's tooling. By adopting a hardened SaaS starter kit, you inherit the architecture of a senior engineer, allowing you to bypass the infrastructure 'death valley' and focus entirely on the product that will land you your first job.

steps.title: Initialize with Antigravity
steps.description: Standardize your project structure immediately to avoid configuration drift.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready Next.js SaaS boilerplate with Supabase integration, Stripe authentication, and Tailwind CSS. Ensure strict TypeScript typing and modular folder structure.

steps.title: Automate Environment Variables
steps.description: Stop hardcoding keys. Use Claude Code to secure your secrets.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my project to use a .env.example file and Zod-based environment variable validation to prevent runtime crashes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'audit env variables and implement zod validation'

steps.title: Configure Database Migrations
steps.description: Ensure your database schema is version-controlled and deployment-ready.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a migration script for a standard SaaS user profile schema and subscription status table.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create supabase migration for user roles and subscription tiers'

steps.title: Setup CI/CD Pipeline
steps.description: Automate deployment so you never have to manually push code again.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, testing, and deployment to Vercel on every push to main.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate github-actions workflow for vercel deployment'

steps.title: Smoke Testing and Launch
steps.description: Perform an end-to-end audit of your auth and payment flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Playwright test script to verify that a user can sign up and reach the dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'write playwright test for user authentication flow'

pro_tips: Always use a 'staging' environment variable set that mirrors production to catch config errors early.
Never commit your .env file; use a centralized secret manager for team projects.
Automate your database schema syncs within your build command to prevent drift between local and prod.
Use feature flags to hide incomplete modules so you can deploy to production daily.
Monitor your deployment logs using an external service like Sentry to identify issues before users do.
content.next_steps: Once your first deployment is live, focus on building a 'feedback loop'—implementing analytics and a simple contact form to hear from your first real users.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer a student just learning—you are an AI builder with a production-ready application. The infrastructure hurdle is cleared; now it is time to scale your vision. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
